name: Lakes of Léon and Soustons Loop
name_original: Réserve Naturelle du Courant d'Huchet - boucle
description: This moderate 34.1-mile (54.8 km) road cycling loop explores the diverse landscapes around the Natural Reserve of Courant d'Huchet.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Soustons benefit from the region's diverse landscapes, characterized by a network of lakes, the expansive Landes forest, and proximity to the Atlantic coast. The terrain is generally flat, offering extensive paved cycling paths often set away from main roads. This area is also a key segment of the Vélodyssée route, providing well-maintained surfaces through varied natural settings. Best road cycling routes around Soustons The most popular road cycling route is Lakes of Léon and Soustons Loop, a 34.1 miles (54.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route navigates through the scenic areas around the Lakes of Léon and Soustons, offering views of the water and surrounding forests. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Baillotte Greenway – West Lake of Soustons loop from Soustons, a moderate 31.4 miles (50.5 km) path. This route features a greenway and circumnavigates the western part of Soustons Lake, providing a serene riding experience through natural landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Port of Capbreton – Odyssée bicycle path loop from Soustons, a 63.7 miles (102.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and along the Vélodyssée, often completed in about 4 hours. Road cycling around Soustons is defined by its extensive network of cycling paths, the tranquil lakes, and the shaded Landes forest. The region offers options for various ability levels, from easy lakeside loops to longer routes along the coast. The routes in Soustons are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Soustons's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Soustons?
A: The road cycling routes around Soustons are generally flat, making them accessible for various fitness levels. You'll find extensive paved cycling paths, often set away from main roads, winding through diverse landscapes including the vast Landes forest, around the area's five lakes, and along the Atlantic coast. The region is known for its low traffic, impeccable surfaces, and ample width, ensuring a relaxing ride.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Soustons area?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes available around Soustons, catering to a range of abilities. The komoot community has explored these routes over 1000 times, highlighting the area's popularity among cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Soustons offers several easy road cycling routes. The terrain is generally flat, with many paths suitable for a relaxed pace. For example, the 4 km cycle path encircling the marine lake of Port d'Albret is ideal for families. While specific 'easy' routes are not detailed in the guide, the overall flat nature and dedicated paths make many options family-friendly.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: While the terrain is generally flat, some routes offer longer distances for a more challenging experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41148558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soustons Pond – Saint-Girons-Plage Cycle Path loop from Soustons&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 186 km route. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41719168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Capbreton – Odyssée bicycle path loop from Soustons&lt;/a&gt; covers over 100 km, providing a longer ride along coastal areas and the Vélodyssée.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Soustons?
A: Soustons is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the area's five lakes, including the large Soustons Lake and the marine lake of Port d'Albret. The routes also take you through the expansive Landes forest. Notable natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3467910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;West Lake of Soustons&lt;/a&gt; and the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6068030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang Noir Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2723709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Courant de Messanges&lt;/a&gt; is also a beautiful feature you might encounter on the Léon-Soustons Trail.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Soustons?
A: Yes, Soustons offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938731781/lakes-of-leon-and-soustons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of Léon and Soustons Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through scenic areas around both lakes. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41470104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Baillotte Greenway – West Lake of Soustons loop from Soustons&lt;/a&gt;, offering a serene ride around the western part of Soustons Lake.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Soustons?
A: Soustons is a great destination for road cycling throughout much of the year. The region benefits from a mild climate. While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the extensive network of shaded paths through the Landes forest makes it enjoyable even during warmer months. The Vélodyssée stretch between Soustons and Vieux-Boucau-les-Bains, for example, winds through shaded pine forests.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Soustons?
A: The road cycling routes in Soustons are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, the extensive network of well-maintained paved paths, and the tranquil atmosphere away from main roads. The blend of lakes, forests, and coastal views provides a varied and enjoyable riding experience.

Q: Are there any unique landmarks or points of interest along the cycling paths?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can discover unique points of interest. A notable feature is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3542414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artistic Sculptures on the Vélodyssée Cycle Path&lt;/a&gt; between Soustons and Vieux-Boucau-les-Bains, which enhance the cycling experience through the shaded pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6988445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capbreton Port and Town Center&lt;/a&gt; can also be explored on longer routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Soustons?
A: While specific parking locations are not detailed, Soustons is well-equipped for visitors. Given its popularity as an outdoor destination, you can generally find parking in and around the town center, near the lakes, or at access points to the main cycling paths. Many routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41159110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camin de Hé: Vielle - Linxe – Winding bicycle path loop from Soustons&lt;/a&gt;, often start from convenient locations within or near Soustons.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes in Soustons?
A: Soustons and its surrounding areas, particularly near the lakes and coastal towns, offer various cafes and restaurants. The marine lake of Port d'Albret, shared with Vieux-Boucau, and Soustons Lake itself are hubs for activities and amenities. You'll find opportunities to stop for refreshments in towns and villages along the routes, especially on popular paths like the Vélodyssée.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike loop?
faqs.answer: This racebike loop is approximately 54.8 km long and generally takes around 2 hours and 11 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for this cycling route, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a loop, so you can start at various points. Specific parking information isn't provided in the tour data, but areas around Moliets-et-Maa or Léon, which are often part of these loops, typically offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability near your intended starting point.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. While much of the area's cycling infrastructure, like sections of the Vélodyssée, is largely flat and paved, some segments might include unpaved or sandy paths, especially closer to the natural reserve. This could make it challenging for road bikes or very young children. For a fully easy experience, consider sticking to the main paved cycle paths.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: You'll primarily encounter paved surfaces, typical for racebike routes in the region. The overall elevation gain is around 265 meters, so it's mostly flat with gentle undulations. However, be aware that some sections, particularly those venturing closer to the lake's southern path or the reserve's perimeter, might have unpaved or sandy stretches that could require dismounting and pushing a road bike.

faqs.question: What natural features or scenery will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: This route offers diverse and beautiful landscapes. You'll cycle through the Landes forest, pine forests, and marshes. The highlight is the proximity to the Natural Reserve of Courant d'Huchet, often called the 'Amazon of the Landes,' with its lush canopy and unique ecosystem where the river flows from Lake Léon to the Atlantic. You'll experience a transition from freshwater environments to coastal dunes.

faqs.question: Are there any notable highlights or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes several interesting points. You'll experience a Quality green route, and sections like the Route de campagne Lette Blanche - Moliets-et-Maa. You'll also ride along the Cycle Path North of Étang de Léon and parts of the Léon-Soustons trail.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route integrates with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of La Vélodyssée, a major long-distance cycling route. It also includes parts of the Piste Cyclable Forêt Saint-Girons Plage, Piste Cyclable - Léon-Soustons, and Lo Camin de Hé, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for cycling near the Natural Reserve of Courant d'Huchet?
faqs.answer: The Courant d'Huchet is a nationally classified Natural Reserve, established for its unique heritage. While cycling routes often skirt its perimeter, direct road cycling *within* the core natural reserve trails is generally not permitted or practical due to terrain and protection efforts. There are no entrance fees for the reserve itself, but specific regulations apply to protect its biodiversity. For deeper exploration of the reserve's interior, consider designated hiking trails or traditional flat-bottom boat tours.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the general cycling paths in the area are often dog-friendly, specific regulations may apply within the Natural Reserve of Courant d'Huchet to protect wildlife. It's best to keep dogs on a leash and check local signage for any restrictions, especially when near sensitive natural areas.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region is enjoyable for cycling outside of peak season to avoid crowds, especially if some routes are described as having difficulties or being more hilly. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and fewer visitors, making them ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes without the summer heat or winter chill.

faqs.question: Are there places to find refreshments or amenities near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through or near towns like Moliets-et-Maa and Léon, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and other amenities. These towns are good spots to refuel and take a break during your ride.
